name: Iterative Inorder with Early Stop
summary: Inorder-traverse the BST with an explicit stack; the values come out ascending, so stop the moment the kth node is popped.
visualizationIntent: init: build the BST from the level-order array — nodes pop in and connect to their parents; the explicit stack starts empty and cur sits at the root. loop: repeatedly dive left, pushing every node onto the stack (frontier), then pop the top as the next-smallest node (visited). check: after each pop, count increments and is compared to k. update: when count reaches k, the popped node's value flashes as the answer and the traversal stops early. move: on a non-match, cur steps to the popped node's right child to continue the inorder walk. return: the kth-smallest value is shown; every node popped before it reads as visited.

lineExplanations.3: Count how many array slots describe the tree.
lineExplanations.4: Start an empty list of the real tree nodes.
lineExplanations.5: Start a lookup from a node's id to the node itself.
lineExplanations.6: Start a status list (0 = unseen, 1 = on the stack, 2 = visited) — one entry per slot.
lineExplanations.7: Loop over every slot to give it a starting status.
lineExplanations.9: child walks the array, handing out children to each real node in order.
lineExplanations.10: Loop over every array slot to build the nodes.
lineExplanations.11: Skip null slots — only real values become nodes.
lineExplanations.12: Create this node with its id, value, and empty (None) child links.
lineExplanations.13: If there's an array slot left to be a left child…
lineExplanations.15: link it as this node's left child.
lineExplanations.16: Consume that slot whether or not it became a child.
lineExplanations.17: If there's an array slot left to be a right child…
lineExplanations.19: link it as this node's right child.
lineExplanations.21: Add the finished node to the node list.
lineExplanations.22: Record it in the id-to-node lookup.
lineExplanations.23: Create the empty stack that drives the inorder walk.
lineExplanations.24: Start cur at the root (id 0); None means 'no node'.
lineExplanations.25: count tracks how many nodes we've popped in ascending order.
lineExplanations.26: answer holds the result; -1 until the kth node is found.
lineExplanations.27: Keep going while there's a node to descend into or the stack still holds ancestors.
lineExplanations.28: Dive left: while there's a current node…
lineExplanations.29: …push it so we can return to it after its left subtree,
lineExplanations.30: mark that pushed node as on-the-stack (frontier),
lineExplanations.31: then move to its left child.
lineExplanations.32: Pop the top — it's the next node in ascending (inorder) order.
lineExplanations.34: One more node visited in sorted order.
lineExplanations.35: If this is the kth node we've popped…
lineExplanations.37: Stop early — no need to traverse the rest of the tree.
lineExplanations.38: Not the kth yet — continue into this node's right subtree.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: is the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def kthSmallest(self, values, k): defines the method; values is the level-order array (null = missing node), k is 1-indexed.
syntaxExplanations.4: [] creates the list of node objects.
syntaxExplanations.5: {} creates an empty dictionary keyed by node id.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(n): loops i over each slot index.
syntaxExplanations.8: state_by_id.append(0) adds an 'unseen' marker for slot i.
syntaxExplanations.9: child = 1 starts the child-slot walker just after the root.
syntaxExplanations.10: for i in range(n): loops over the slots again to build nodes.
syntaxExplanations.11: values[i] is not None skips the null placeholders.
syntaxExplanations.12: A dict literal storing id, value, and left/right child links (None for now).
syntaxExplanations.13: child < n checks there's a slot left to be a left child.
syntaxExplanations.14: values[child] is not None checks that slot holds a real node.
syntaxExplanations.15: node["left"] = child records the left child's id.
syntaxExplanations.16: child = child + 1 moves past this slot.
syntaxExplanations.17: child < n checks there's a slot left to be a right child.
syntaxExplanations.18: values[child] is not None checks that slot holds a real node.
syntaxExplanations.19: node["right"] = child records the right child's id.
syntaxExplanations.20: child = child + 1 moves past this slot.
syntaxExplanations.21: nodes.append(node) adds the built node to the list.
syntaxExplanations.22: by_id[i] = node stores it under its id for fast lookup.
syntaxExplanations.24: cur = 0 if n > 0 else None sets the cursor to the root, or None for an empty tree.
syntaxExplanations.25: count = 0 starts the count of nodes popped in sorted order.
syntaxExplanations.26: answer = -1 sets a sentinel; the real answer overwrites it.
syntaxExplanations.27: while cur is not None or len(stack) > 0: runs while nodes remain to visit.
syntaxExplanations.28: while cur is not None: keeps descending as long as there's a node.
syntaxExplanations.29: stack.append(cur) pushes the current node to return to later.
syntaxExplanations.30: state_by_id[cur] = 1 marks the pushed node as on-the-stack.
syntaxExplanations.31: cur = by_id[cur]["left"] moves to the left child (None if none).
syntaxExplanations.32: cur = stack.pop() takes the most recently pushed node — the next in order.
syntaxExplanations.33: state_by_id[cur] = 2 marks the popped node visited.
syntaxExplanations.34: count = count + 1 records another node visited in ascending order.
syntaxExplanations.35: if count == k: tests whether this is the kth-smallest node.
syntaxExplanations.36: answer = by_id[cur]["value"] reads the kth-smallest value.
syntaxExplanations.37: break exits the loop early — the answer is found.
syntaxExplanations.38: cur = by_id[cur]["right"] moves into the right subtree to continue inorder.
syntaxExplanations.39: return answer hands back the kth-smallest value.
